title: sempervivum rupestre
intro: Sempervivum rupestre, also known as houseleek or living stone, is a succulent plant species that belongs to the succulent family, Crassulaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various skin issues and wounds due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The plant is rich in health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide protection against radiation damage. The herbal preparations of Sempervivum rupestre include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to benefit from its medicinal properties.
